seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with Flowise + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with Flowise + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with Flowise enables rapid deployment of code-aware LLM agents that analyze repositories, generate fixes, and validate solutions through drag-and-drop chatflows. However, unvalidated code generation, silent tool failures, and context loss in multi-agent handoffs can lead to insecure or incorrect code recommendations. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify code correctness, scan for vulnerabilities, and ensure safe execution across the entire Flowise pipeline.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with Flowise
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Flowise AI Coding Assistant uses a multi-node chatflow starting with a Vector Store retriever that pulls relevant code files from a repository embedding store. This feeds into an LLM agent node configured with coding-specific prompts, followed by custom tool nodes for sandbox code execution and test validation. A multi-agent supervisor orchestrates iterative debugging loops, passing context through sequential handoffs until validation passes or escalation occurs.

Key components include a GitHub Repo Loader → Chroma Vector Store → OpenAI Chat Model with function calling → PythonREPL Tool (sandboxed) → Test Runner Node → Final Validator Agent. Failures often occur at intermediate retrieval (low-relevance docs), tool execution (silent crashes), and agent handoffs (lost context).

Vex integrates as a global callback handler, intercepting outputs at each node to validate code semantics, security, and compliance before propagation.

risks.name: Unvalidated Code Generation
risks.description: LLM produces syntactically correct but semantically wrong or vulnerable code without test validation
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic analysis + test execution verification at agent output

risks.name: Vector Retrieval Drift
risks.description: Irrelevant repository files retrieved due to embedding degradation, leading to hallucinated fixes
risks.mitigation: Vex relevance scoring on retrieved documents before LLM injection

risks.name: Sandbox Escape Attempts
risks.description: Generated code contains malicious payloads attempting credential extraction or system compromise
risks.mitigation: Vex security scanning + AST analysis before sandbox execution

risks.name: Context Loss in Agent Handoffs
risks.description: Multi-agent debugging loses repository context between supervisor → executor → validator
risks.mitigation: Vex context continuity checks across sequential Flowise node outputs

implementation.title: Flowise Chatflow with Vex Callback Handler
implementation.description: Configure the main chatflow with Vex as the global callback to intercept all node outputs for validation

implementation.title: Repository Context Retriever with Relevance Check
implementation.description: Enhance vector store node with Vex relevance scoring to filter low-quality retrievals

implementation.title: Secure Sandbox Tool Node
implementation.description: Wrap PythonREPL tool with Vex security scanning before execution

implementation.title: Test Validation Agent
implementation.description: Final agent validates generated code executes successfully with tests
